csf_subcategory_name: Policy for managing cybersecurity risks is established based on organizational context and priorities
Organizational leadership is responsible and accountable for cybersecurity risk
Risk management objectives are established and agreed to by organizational stakeholders
A cybersecurity supply chain risk management program is established
Inventories of hardware managed by the organization are maintained
Inventories of software and systems managed by the organization are maintained
Assets are prioritized based on classification and criticality
Vulnerabilities in assets are identified and recorded
Threats and vulnerabilities are used to understand inherent risk and prioritize response
Identities and credentials for authorized users and services are managed
Users and services are authenticated
The confidentiality and integrity of data-at-rest are protected
The confidentiality and integrity of data-in-transit are protected
Backups of data are created and tested
Networks and environments are protected from unauthorized logical access
Networks and network services are monitored to find potentially adverse events
Computing hardware and software are monitored to find potentially adverse events
Potentially adverse events are analyzed to understand associated activities
Incidents are declared when adverse events meet defined incident criteria
The incident response plan is executed in coordination with relevant third parties
Internal and external stakeholders are notified of incidents
Incidents are contained
Incidents are eradicated
The recovery portion of the incident response plan is executed
Recovery activities and progress are communicated to stakeholders
